assertions.description: VideoFrame is exposed — the WebCodecs interface the new metadata() lives on.
VideoFrame.prototype.metadata is a function — the call returns a dictionary that may carry rtpTimestamp.
VideoFrame.prototype.metadata() returns an object even for a frame with no native metadata — an empty dictionary in that case.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const c=new OffscreenCanvas(1,1);c.getContext('2d').fillRect(0,0,1,1);const f=new VideoFrame(c,{timestamp:0});const m=f.metadata();f.close();return typeof m==='object'&&m!==null;}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: MediaStreamTrackProcessor is exposed — the bridge from WebRTC tracks to VideoFrames, the source of rtpTimestamp-carrying frames.
RTCPeerConnection is exposed — the WebRTC entry point whose received frames carry rtpTimestamp.
